questions.question: The function f(x) = 3sin(2x) has a period of what value?
questions.explanation: For y = sin(Bx), the period is 2π/B. Here B = 2, so period = 2π/2 = π.

questions.question: In triangle ABC, angle A = 40°, angle B = 60°, and side a = 10. Find side b using the Law of Sines. Round to the nearest tenth.
questions.explanation: By Law of Sines: a/sin(A) = b/sin(B). So 10/sin(40°) = b/sin(60°). Thus b = 10·sin(60°)/sin(40°) ≈ 10·(0.866)/0.643 ≈ 12.1.

questions.question: What is the vertical shift and direction of the graph y = cos(x) - 4?
questions.explanation: The constant term -4 outside the trig function translates the entire graph vertically. A negative constant moves it downward.

questions.question: In triangle PQR, sides p = 7, q = 9, and angle R = 50°. Use the Law of Cosines to find side r. Round to the nearest tenth.
questions.explanation: Law of Cosines: r² = p² + q² - 2pq·cos(R). So r² = 49 + 81 - 2(7)(9)cos(50°) ≈ 130 - 126(0.643) ≈ 50.2. Thus r ≈ 7.1.

questions.question: The graph of y = -2sin(x) has an amplitude of what value?
questions.explanation: Amplitude is the absolute value of the coefficient in front of the sine function: |−2| = 2. The negative sign reflects the graph but doesn't change amplitude.

questions.question: In triangle XYZ, x = 5, y = 8, and z = 10. Use the Law of Cosines to find the measure of angle Z in degrees. Round to the nearest tenth.
questions.explanation: Law of Cosines: z² = x² + y² - 2xy·cos(Z). So 100 = 25 + 64 - 80·cos(Z), giving cos(Z) = −11/80 ≈ −0.1375. Thus Z = arccos(−0.1375) ≈ 78.5°.
